body_txt: In order to make them the underdog , more relatable to the audience or for some other reason, a character is portrayed as coming from average beginnings and being relatively anonymous before the plot happens. They can be specifically shown to be poor, downtrodden or outcasts to make this happen. They may even be Loser Protagonists . So what better way to distinguish The Rival right off the bat than by having them be just the opposite? Depending on genre, they can be anywhere from royalty, to having a famous family member, or just plain being loaded—with all the advantages that implies. Of course, this also makes said rival more of a challenge for the hero, which in turn makes the victory that much sweeter. It is more common when the character in question is younger, because what your parents do reflects more on your personal story at that point. This trope also allows the rival to start off privileged and grow from looking down on to respecting the hero as he climbs up the ladder himself. Subtrope of The Rival . See also Alpha Bitch , Rich Bitch , The Evil Prince or Opposing Sports Team . Can become Rich Suitor, Poor Suitor if there's romance involved. When applied to two entire rival organizations, can overlap with Evil Counterpart Organization . Compare Bad Guys Have Better Budgets for situations where the antagonist is inherently evil and/or villainous. Contrast Villainous Underdog .
